subject_profile.summary: Base country-level assessment; default category weights prioritize rule of law, institutional integrity, and personal freedom.
stated_values: Rule of law and institutional integrity weighted most heavily
Personal freedom and security from arbitrary state action are foundational
Economic success is contingent on rule of law, not a substitute for it

flags: Generated 2026-05-28 under v2.0: governance/rights/politics rebuilt from independent sources (IMF, World Bank, V-Dem, RSF, Freedom House, HRW, Amnesty, OHCHR, UNHCR, CRS) + international press; Ugandan state media EXCLUDED there, with economics admitted via IMF.
Politics: a leader in power since 1986 re-elected in January 2026 amid an opposition crackdown, with his son positioned to succeed - a managed/suppressed stability with a violent succession fat-tail.

Standout drags: military-influenced weak rule of law, a draconian anti-LGBT law and opposition repression, recurrent disease outbreaks, and ADF/ISIS-linked conflict; offsets are low inflation and a generous refugee-host record.

economic.notes: Solid growth and low inflation, against a widening deficit and oil/commodity concentration.

fiscal_state.notes: A deficit widened to ~6% GDP with debt ~52%, the IMF urging discipline as oil nears - manageable, not distressed. (IMF 2026)

monetary_independence.notes: A managed shilling with a credible-but-limited-independence central bank, stable on coffee/FX inflows. (Bank of Uganda)

reserve_currency_intl_monetary.notes: The shilling is not a reserve currency. (definitional)

inflation.notes: ~3.6% and below target - a genuine relative strength. (Bank of Uganda; IMF)

banking_stability.notes: Developing and sound-ish, with falling NPLs. (Bank of Uganda)

trade_actions_capacity.notes: Strong ~6% growth on coffee/gold plus an oil project (the EACOP pipeline, first oil slipped), with commodity/oil concentration and EACOP climate-finance controversy. (IMF; IEEFA)

institutional.notes: Military-influenced weak rule of law and a heavily pressured press.

rule_of_law.notes: Patronage and no real judicial independence, with a 2025 law restoring military trials of civilians over the Supreme Court. (HRW; OHCHR; WJP)

statistical_integrity.notes: Usable and IMF-engaged; political-domain data unreliable. (IMF)

press_freedom.notes: A poor RSF standing, with election-period beatings/detentions and internet shutdowns. (RSF; Freedom House)

political_social.notes: Entrenched rule with a succession fat-tail and restricted liberties, against a generous refugee-host record.

political_stability.notes: A leader in power since 1986 re-elected in Jan 2026 amid an internet shutdown, killings, and opposition house arrest, with his son positioned to succeed - a violent succession fat-tail. (Al Jazeera; HRW)

civil_liberties.notes: A 2023 Anti-Homosexuality Act (upheld, with widespread abuse) and NGO/opposition repression. (HRW)

treatment_non_citizens.notes: Africa's largest refugee host (~1.8M) with a progressive land/movement policy - a genuine positive - though an authoritarian ceiling for others. (UNHCR)

social_cohesion.notes: Managed ethnic/regional cleavages with a northern-conflict legacy. (V-Dem)

demographics.notes: Among the world's youngest and fastest-growing - a dividend-or-Malthusian double-edge. (World Bank)

geopolitical.notes: A Western security partner drifting and rights-pressured, with active ADF/regional conflict.

alliance_reliability.notes: A Western counterterror/peacekeeping partner pivoting toward China/Russia and pressured on rights. (CRS)

conflict_involvement.notes: ADF/ISIS-linked attacks, a DRC intervention, and South-Sudan-border deployment. (Al Jazeera; UPDF)

sanctions_capital_controls.notes: World Bank lending was frozen over the anti-LGBT law (resumed 2025) and US aid cut; otherwise an open capital account. (Bloomberg; CRS)

physical_practical.notes: Improving but poor infrastructure, weak healthcare with recurrent outbreaks, and climate exposure.

climate.notes: Landslides, drought-flood whiplash, Lake Victoria variability, a rain-fed economy, weak readiness. (ND-GAIN; WB)

healthcare.notes: Weak and low-spend, with recurrent Ebola/Marburg outbreaks and US-aid-cut clinic closures. (WHO; CNN)

infrastructure.notes: Improving (oil roads, dam power) but poor-moderate and landlocked. (World Bank)

summary: A fast-growing economy with notably low inflation and Africa's largest, most progressive refugee-host record, undercut by entrenched authoritarian governance: a leader in power since 1986 re-elected in January 2026 amid an opposition crackdown and internet shutdown, his son positioned to succeed, a 2025 law restoring military trials of civilians, a draconian anti-LGBT law, and a heavily pressured press. Recurrent Ebola/Marburg outbreaks, ADF/ISIS-linked conflict and a DRC intervention, and oil/EACOP-concentration risks round out a negative profile.
